author: framework
rationale: Weak baseline deterministic demo with rough language and vague planning.

author: proposer
rationale: Primary failure is chart quality rather than task mismatch. A stronger note model plus a more structured note-generation style should improve factual retention, sectioning, and professional tone without changing the pipeline shape.

diff.description: Use a stronger note writer with a chart-ready structured style.

author: proposer
rationale: The artifact suggests facts were lost before note writing. Relaxing overly aggressive preprocessing should preserve more timeline, meds, allergy, and return-precaution details from choppy audio.

diff.description: Preserve more speech content by making preprocessing less lossy.

author: proposer
rationale: This case needs both better transcript preservation and stricter note formatting. Combining less lossy preprocessing with a stronger structured generator is the highest-upside variant for factual capture and clinical polish.

diff.description: Joint upgrade for noisy ASR inputs and chart-ready note output.

author: proposer
rationale: The main failure is that the note is too brief, leaks pipeline meta-commentary, and omits core clinical sections. A stronger note model with a more detailed note style is the most direct way to improve completeness, tone, and chart-readiness without changing upstream audio handling.

diff.description: Use a stronger note generator and a fuller clinical note style.

author: proposer
rationale: Some rubric misses look like dropped or weakened source facts, especially timeline, medications, allergy, and precautions. Relaxing overly aggressive preprocessing should preserve more usable transcript detail for the downstream note writer.

diff.description: Reduce information loss in preprocessing to preserve more clinical detail for note generation.

author: proposer
rationale: The best next high-upside variant combines better transcript retention with a stronger, more structured note writer. This directly targets the repeated failures on factual capture, structure, precautions, and clinical style.

diff.description: Combine gentler audio preprocessing with a stronger structured note generator.

author: proposer
rationale: The largest failures are missing sections, weak plan detail, and non-clinical wording. Moving note generation away from a brief style should improve structure, completeness, and chart-ready tone without changing the pipeline shape.

diff.description: Structured clinical note generation focused on complete HPI, assessment, plan, and return precautions.

author: proposer
rationale: The note is omitting many encounter facts, which may partly come from overly harsh preprocessing. Preserving more audio detail should improve factual capture before the note generator formats the result.

diff.description: Recall-oriented preprocessing to retain more symptoms, meds, allergy, and plan details from the audio.

author: proposer
rationale: The strongest candidate is a combined variant that improves both transcript fidelity and note synthesis. This directly targets the observed failures in factual capture, completeness, clinical style, and precautions.

diff.description: Higher-fidelity preprocessing plus structured clinical note generation for fuller, cleaner outpatient notes.

author: proposer
rationale: The main failure is note-generation behavior: the output leaks pipeline/demo commentary, lacks SOAP-style structure, and omits plan/precautions. Tightening the note prompt toward a chart-ready clinical format is the lowest-risk first move without changing the overall pipeline.

diff.description: Structured clinical note prompt that forbids pipeline/meta commentary and requires assessment, plan, and return precautions.

author: proposer
rationale: The note is missing concrete facts that likely dropped earlier in the pipeline. Relaxing overly aggressive preprocessing should preserve more timeline, meds, allergy, negatives, and return-precaution details before note generation.

diff.description: Recall-oriented preprocessing with less aggressive trimming and VAD plus light enhancement.

author: proposer
rationale: The strongest combined bet is to improve both factual retention and final note quality: preserve more source detail upstream and use a more capable note generator with a structured clinical style downstream.

diff.description: Higher-fidelity pipeline for chart-ready follow-up notes with better retention and stronger generation.

author: proposer
rationale: The main failure is note-generation style: the output is too brief, leaks pipeline commentary, and omits standard clinical sections. Tightening the note prompt toward a chart-ready SOAP/HPI-style note should improve completeness and tone without changing the rest of the pipeline.

diff.description: Chart-ready clinical note prompt with explicit sectioning and no transcript/pipeline meta language.

author: proposer
rationale: Several misses look upstream as well: aggressive trimming and strict VAD can drop low-volume details like negatives, meds, allergy, and precautions. Relaxing preprocessing while keeping the same generator should improve factual capture before note synthesis.

diff.description: Higher-recall audio preprocessing to preserve low-volume clinical details for downstream note generation.

author: proposer
rationale: If the task is to turn noisy transcript output into a polished outpatient note, model capacity is likely part of the bottleneck. A stronger note-generation model paired with a more explicit clinical style should improve fidelity, plan specificity, and presentation.

diff.description: Upgrade note generation model and prompt for fuller clinical synthesis and cleaner plan/precautions.
